    "title":"`branch != main` preflight is incompatible with documented plugin workflow",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":311,
      "symbol":"1.12b DoD preflight",
      "snippet":"Preflight enforces: cwd matches plan's `repo` field, branch != main, permissions present, beads ready state clean."
    },
    "recommendation":"Weaken to a warning with `--allow-main` override, or parametrize the forbidden-branch list per preset. The marketplace repo itself defaults to `main` for PRs (gitStatus: 'Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): main'), and operators routinely run /swarm-do:do against plan files living on `main`. A hard reject on this condition will block the dogfood cohort the promotion scorecard depends on (line 374).",
    "confidence":0.9,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-branch-main-hardblock"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-02",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"bug",
    "title":"Preflight requires a plan `repo` field that does not exist on current plans",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":311,
      "symbol":"1.12b DoD preflight",
      "snippet":"cwd matches plan's `repo` field"
    },
    "recommendation":"Spec is validating a field that isn't declared anywhere in the plan grammar or on the plan file being reviewed. Either (a) add `repo` to the plan schema in 1.12b and cover a migration for every plan under plans/ and research/, or (b) fall back to `git remote get-url origin` when the field is absent. Without one of these, preflight will hard-fail on 100% of existing plans on day one.",
    "confidence":0.9,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-missing-repo-field"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-03",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"maintainability",
    "title":"\"beads ready state clean\" is undefined — ambiguous preflight gate",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":311,
      "symbol":"1.12b DoD preflight",
      "snippet":"beads ready state clean"
    },
    "recommendation":"Define the assertion concretely (e.g. `bd ready` returns non-empty AND no in_progress issue owned by swarm-do roles). As written, 'clean' could be interpreted as zero in_progress or zero blocked — the former would block any session where a prior swarm phase is still running in another worktree. Cross-reference beads-preflight.sh:15-48 which only checks rig presence; 1.12b needs a second helper with its own contract.",
    "confidence":0.85,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-beads-clean-undefined"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-04",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"security",
    "title":"`swarm permissions install` has no defined confirmation mechanism and no uninstall/rollback path",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":217,
      "symbol":"1.12-N2",
      "snippet":"performs a structural JSON parse/merge with an atomic write ... on operator confirmation"
    },
    "recommendation":"The existing CLI (swarm-do/py/swarm_do/pipeline/cli.py) is pure argparse with no stdin prompting anywhere. 'On operator confirmation' needs either an explicit `--yes` flag, a TTY prompt (and non-TTY refuse), and a matching `swarm permissions uninstall --role <r>` that reverses the merge via the backup. Without rollback the backup file is the only recovery surface and rots silently on second install. Also specify backup retention policy (overwrite vs rotate).",
    "confidence":0.9,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-install-no-confirm-no-rollback"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-05",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"maintainability",
    "title":"Permissions JSON fragment schema and merge-conflict policy unspecified",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":211,
      "symbol":"1.12-N2 bundles",
      "snippet":"JSON fragments describing the tool allowlist each role needs."
    },
    "recommendation":"Plan doesn't pin: (1) the Claude settings schema version the fragment targets, (2) whether fragments declare `permissions.allow`/`.deny`/`.ask` and how overlapping entries resolve (union, precedence, error), (3) how `additionalDirectories` / env / hook keys are handled if present. Pick a canonical format (e.g. JSON Patch RFC6902 or a swarm-specific structural merge), write it into 1.12b DoD, and ship an example for one role so 'preserve unrelated keys' has a testable contract.",
    "confidence":0.85,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-fragment-schema-undefined"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-06",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"bug",
    "title":"Role list is inconsistent with the agents catalog — 6 is wrong",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":304,
      "symbol":"1.12b DoD permissions bundles",
      "snippet":"`swarm-do/permissions/*.json` bundles shipped for 6 roles."
    },
    "recommendation":"Plan lists {writer, spec-review, review, research, clarify, codex-review} at 1.12-N2 line 211, but the pipeline dispatches additional roles routinely: agent-analysis, agent-analysis-judge, agent-code-review, agent-code-synthesizer, agent-debug, agent-docs, agent-research-merge, agent-writer-judge (see the agents listed in this repo's agent registry and validation.py _all_roles). Either enumerate every role the default + hybrid-review + competitive presets exercise, or generate the bundle list from the pipeline YAML (cf. providers.py:58 `_stage_roles`). Shipping 6 bundles will leave writer-judge / code-review / docs writers hitting the exact permission blocks 1.12-N2 is meant to eliminate.",
    "confidence":0.9,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-role-list-undercount"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-07",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"maintainability",
    "title":"1.12b bundles two independent risky workstreams; split recommended",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":301,
      "symbol":"1.12b — Preflight + permissions",
      "snippet":"1.12b — Preflight + permissions"
    },
    "recommendation":"Split into 1.12b-permissions (ship bundles + check/install CLI, verify against fixtures, operator opt-in) and 1.12b-preflight (external-deps-check + cwd/branch/beads gates wired into stock pipelines). Permissions ship without mutating orchestrator flow; preflight changes the run entry contract for every preset. Combining them makes the failure blast radius for any one sub-item 'bricks /swarm-do:do', and gates promotion criteria on two orthogonal metrics.",
    "confidence":0.8,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-split-permissions-preflight"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-08",
    "severity":"high",
    "category":"bug",
    "title":"1.12b depends on an unfinished 1.11 deliverable (`external-deps-check`)",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":306,
      "symbol":"1.12b DoD",
      "snippet":"`external-deps-check` stage (from 1.11 metaswarm plan) implemented and wired into stock pipelines."
    },
    "recommendation":"Section header (line 4) already pins 1.11 as a dependency, but 1.12b redefines 'implemented' here — previously it was only planned. Clarify whether 1.12b ships the stage itself or only wires an existing stage. If the former, the SKILL.md preflight (SKILL.md:10-40) and pipeline registry have no stage type for it today; validation.py STAGE_KEYS (line 20) would reject a `external-deps-check` block. Add a concrete schema change task to 1.12b, or gate 1.12b on 1.11 landing external-deps-check first.",
    "confidence":0.8,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-external-deps-hidden-dep"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-09",
    "severity":"medium",
    "category":"security",
    "title":"Atomic write spec omits same-filesystem/symlink/permission preservation constraints",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":217,
      "symbol":"1.12-N2",
      "snippet":"atomic write (`settings.local.json.tmp` + rename)"
    },
    "recommendation":"Pin: (1) tempfile must live in the same directory as the target to make rename atomic on POSIX; (2) follow symlinks or refuse (user may have settings.local.json symlinked into dotfiles repo); (3) preserve original mode/uid/gid; (4) windows fallback (os.replace); (5) fsync before rename. Current phrasing will get implemented as `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile()` (default dir /tmp) which fails the atomicity guarantee on different filesystems.",
    "confidence":0.8,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-atomic-write-underspecified"
  },
  {
    "finding_id":"1.12b-10",
    "severity":"medium",
    "category":"maintainability",
    "title":"No `swarm permissions install --dry-run` specified",
    "evidence":{
      "file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md",
      "line":217,
      "symbol":"1.12-N2",
      "snippet":"`swarm permissions install --role writer`"
    },
    "recommendation":"`check` prints the merged stanza, `install` writes. Add `--dry-run` on `install` that prints the exact diff that would be written without touching disk. The plan already suggests `check` does most of this; clarifying the boundary (`check` = detection only; `install --dry-run` = final diff) prevents operators from running `install` when all they want is the preview. Mirrors `bin/swarm preset dry-run` ergonomics already present in cli.py:159.",
    "confidence":0.75,
    "fingerprint":"1.12b-install-needs-dry-run"
  }
]}
